transcript: Oh dear. Now at six, a terrifying carjacking caught on camera. A woman crashing into cars and nearly running people over in a grocery store parking lot. Good evening, everyone. I'm Pat Harvey. Let's get straight to this developing story in our Lauren Posen live from the scene in Agoura Hills. Lauren, this video is just unbelievable. Pat, unbelievable and really scary. In fact, there's still some debris just like this on the ground from when the woman crashed the car. We spoke with a witness who works right over here at Union Bank. He says he saw the entire thing go down. He says it started when the woman approached the victim in her car asking for a ride. He says, well, she said no, and that's when the suspect got into the car and took off. All this ending, Pat, right over here, and again, there's still a little bit of debris that you can see from the cars. I want to show you some video because that really helps tell this story. Take a look. that's when people run out of the grocery store to try to help the victim and get the suspect out of the car people start screaming and panicking and this is when things really escalated just watch Get out of the car! Get out! Get out of the car! Just total chaos there. And all deputies say about five cars were damaged. The stolen car had a club on it, on the steering wheel, those anti-theft devices, making it more difficult to drive. Witnesses tell us they were scared the suspect was going to run them over. Another witness told us the victim is a regular at her beauty shop, and she ran out to help when she heard her scream. She was kind of bouncing like a ping pong ball off the other cars. She tried to bail out of the vehicle. When she did this, she stepped out of the air, slipped, and that's when myself and the other two gentlemen being interviewed got on top of her, tied her hands with a bandana, tied her shoes together, and stayed on top of her until police came to get her. Do you know how many people you almost just killed? And deputies say about five cars in total were damaged. The victims were treated for minor injury. The victim and the suspect were treated for minor injuries, and the suspect was taken into custody and is expected to be booked later today on carjacking charges. Now, back out here live, I want to show you, this is in front of the Ralphs here. And, Pat, you can see some of the indentation here in the wall from when the car hit it earlier today, the suspect taking the victim's car, driving it right into this retaining wall here. So, I mean, just unbelievable. I mean, this is a girl. This is a very quiet neighborhood here. People are telling me this does not happen. I'm going through more of an interview with the gentleman we spoke with at Union Bank. I'll put together that part of the story for you at nine. And he just, he kind of, he reiterates this does not happen here. He could not believe what he was seeing. Pat, we'll send it back to you. Well, the fact that people were not injured more than a minor is remarkable in itself. All right, we'll let you get back to work. Thank you, Lauren.
